heroSubtitle: Based in Athens, Georgia, where academic rigor meets real-world healthcare experience.

story.title: Training that
paragraphs: Healthcare organizations know they need AI, but their teams haven't been trained to use it.

Scale Street combines academic depth from the University of Georgia with hands-on experience from the organizations we serve. The result is training that's grounded in evidence and built for the real world.
Our goal is simple: give your people the skills they need so your organization can move forward on its own terms.

leadership.name: Tyler S. Wallace, Ph.D.
leadership.shortBio: International trainer. UGA faculty. Microsoft Certified Trainer.
paragraphs: Tyler Wallace, Ph.D. is an international trainer who has trained over 2,000 professionals across healthcare, government, and higher education. He brings 15+ years of experience spanning MD Anderson Cancer Center, USAID, and healthcare organizations across the Southeast.
As faculty in the University of Georgia's Master of Health Administration program, Tyler teaches Health Informatics and connects classroom learning with what's happening in the field.

Tyler is a Microsoft Certified Trainer (MCT), delivering official Microsoft AI courses for users, administrators, and builders. He combines deep healthcare knowledge with practical, hands-on instruction that participants consistently rate as the best training they've attended.

values.title: Courage
values.description: To be willing to lead in uncertainty -- to step forward when the path isn't clear and the stakes are real.

values.title: Curiosity
values.description: To explore and question everything -- to stay open, keep learning, and never assume we have it all figured out.

values.title: Growth
values.description: We have to grow as a company and as individuals -- every challenge is an opportunity to get better.

values.title: Grace
values.description: We will mess up. We will extend grace to ourselves and to others -- because that's how trust is built.

approach.title: How We Work
approach.subtitle: Four principles that guide every training.

steps.title: Listen First
steps.description: We start by understanding your organization, your people, and your goals. No off-the-shelf training.

steps.title: Meet Your Team Where They Are
steps.description: We assess current skill levels and design training that builds from where your people actually are.

steps.title: Make It Practical
steps.description: Every session uses real scenarios from your workflows. Your team leaves with skills they can apply immediately.

steps.title: Reinforce and Sustain
steps.description: Training doesn't end in the classroom. We provide coaching and follow-up to drive lasting adoption.

whoWeWorkWith.title: Healthcare organizations
whoWeWorkWith.description: From large health systems to rural critical access hospitals, we train teams that are serious about getting their people ready.

items.title: Health Systems
items.description: Multi-site organizations building skills across clinical and administrative teams.

items.title: Critical Access Hospitals
items.description: Rural hospitals that need training but can't justify a full-time hire to deliver it.

items.title: Federally Qualified Health Centers
items.description: Community health centers equipping staff with the skills to serve underserved populations more effectively.

ctaTitle: We'd Love to Learn About Your Organization.
ctaSubtitle: Let's talk about which training fits your team.
